objective: Add a fleet-wide staleness gate so governed repos cannot let docs/PROGRESS.md backlog sections drift from backlog-labeled GitHub Issues, and surface that drift in the weekly overlord sweep.
scope_boundary: Add a shared validator in governance that compares active PROGRESS backlog sections with backlog-labeled GitHub issues for a target repo.
Wire that validator into the reusable governance check for governed product repos.
Surface the same drift status in the weekly overlord sweep report.
out_of_scope: Auto-editing product repo PROGRESS.md files from governance automation.
Changing the backlog model away from GitHub issues plus PROGRESS mirror sections.
Broad project-management workflow redesign outside the staleness gate.
research_summary: Issue #81 identified a real fleet drift problem: product repos can close or open backlog-labeled GitHub issues without the active backlog sections in docs/PROGRESS.md staying in sync. Existing governance checks only validate structure and co-staging, not issue parity. The right fix is a shared issue-staleness validator that can run in repo CI and in the weekly sweep, using GitHub as the canonical issue source and active PROGRESS sections as the local mirror surface.

sprints.name: Sprint 1
sprints.goal: Implement and validate the PROGRESS ↔ GitHub issue staleness gate.
tasks: Create a shared validator that checks open backlog-labeled GitHub issues are present in active PROGRESS backlog sections and closed backlog-labeled issues are absent from those active sections.
Wire the validator into the reusable governance check for governed product repos.
Add the same validator to the weekly sweep report so fleet backlog drift is visible in the operator report.
acceptance_criteria: The validator fails against the known AIS drift evidence from issue #81.
Reusable governance CI runs the new staleness gate for governed product repos.
Weekly sweep report includes per-repo PROGRESS ↔ GitHub backlog drift status.

specialist_reviews.focus: Keep the fix on staleness detection and reporting, not backlog model redesign.
specialist_reviews.summary: The approved slice uses GitHub issues as canonical and PROGRESS as the governed mirror. The fix is a parity gate plus sweep visibility, not a new planning system.

alternate_model_review.summary: The staleness-gate slice is deterministic governance tooling and does not require alternate-model review.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement the shared PROGRESS↔GitHub issue validator, wire it into reusable governance CI and the weekly sweep, and validate it against the known AIS drift.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Create the validator and run it against the live AIS backlog drift before wiring the reusable workflow and sweep report.

material_deviation_rules: If review surfaces more required work, either finish it in this slice or record it as an issue-backed follow-up before closing.
Do not expand this issue into auto-remediation of product repo PROGRESS files; detection/reporting only.
If repo-specific exceptions are needed, document them explicitly instead of silently exempting repos from the gate.
